title: MAP 6.13: Design resilience controls and chaos tests
description: Wave 7 from MAP 6.13 plan. Design idempotency registry, dead-letter queue, durable/resumable execution, circuit breakers, chaos tests, degradation policy, and dependency DAG support. Read MAP_System/artifacts/planning/map-613-master-implementation-plan.md, MAP_System/SELF_REPAIR_SYSTEM.md, MAP_System/scripts/agent_loop.py, MAP_System/graph/runner.py, MAP_System/scripts/validate_task_graph.py, and Guidelines/6.13/files5/MAP-Simulation-Results-Round4.md before editing.
acceptance_criteria: Resilience spec defines idempotency registry, dead-letter queue, circuit breaker inputs/actions, and blast-radius containment expectations.
Durable execution spec defines checkpointed steps, event replay resume, and idempotency-key safety for partial retries.
Degradation policy defines fail-open/fail-closed behavior for validator, cloud tier, canonical store, local model, and absent operator.
Chaos test plan covers killed agent-loop handler, stale mirror, malformed protocol output, hung agent/reaper, mid-task resume, and committed poisoned-state recovery.
Dependency DAG spec defines decomposer-emitted edges and cycle/unresolved-dependency validation.
